DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF FADI HANANI (TRANSACTION ID # 100002095) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HANANI, FADI
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-23-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAIL HATTER'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS GRANTED. THE MATTER IS TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY. DEFENDANT HAS ESTABLISHED THAT HE RESIDES IN LOS ANGELES COUNTY AND NONE OF THE STATUTORY EXCEPTIONS TO THE GENERAL VENUE RULE APPLY. (SEE CCP SEC. 395(A).) THE CONTRACT DOES NOT SPECIFY WHERE IT IS TO BE PERFORMED. (COMPLAINT, EX. A, PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT.) DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION ESTABLISHES THAT HE ENTERED INTO THE CONTRACT AT HIS HOME IN LOS ANGELES COUNTY. (DECLARATION OF MICHAIL HATTER, PAR. 5.) PLAINTIFF STATES IN HIS DECLARATION THAT THE CONTRACT WAS ACTUALLY ACCEPTED AT PLAINTIFF'S RESIDENCE, BUT HIS DECLARATION IS SILENT ON WHERE HIS RESIDENCE IS LOCATED, AND IS THEREFORE INSUFFICIENT TO REFUTE DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION. (DECLARATION OF FADI HANANI, PAR. 2.) THE PLAIN LANGUAGE OF THE PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT LIMITS THE SELECTION OF SAN FRANCISCO TO ARBITRATION, AND THEREFORE IS IRRELEVANT TO DETERMINING VENUE FOR A COURT ACTION. (COMPLAINT, EX. A, PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT, PAR. 19.). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE SUZANNE R. BOLANOS; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; DEPT. 302. (302/SRB)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-10-2015 Continued To Sep-09-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Jul-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MICHAIL HATTER'S Motion For Change Of Venue is granted. The matter is transferred to Los Angeles County. Defendant has established that he resides in Los Angeles County and none of the statutory exceptions to the general venue rule apply. (See CCP sec. 395(a).) The contract does not specify where it is to be performed. (Complaint, Ex. A, Partnership Agreement.) Defendant's declaration establishes that he entered into the contract at his home in Los Angeles County. (Declaration of Michail Hatter, par. 5.) Plaintiff states in his declaration that the contract was actually accepted at Plaintiff's residence, but his declaration is silent on where his residence is located, and is therefore insufficient to refute Defendant's declaration. (Declaration of Fadi Hanani, par. 2.) The plain language of the Partnership Agreement limits the selection of San Francisco to arbitration, and therefore is irrelevant to determining venue for a court action. (Complaint, Ex. A, Partnership Agreement, par. 19.). Order signed. Judge Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/SRB)
